intent: Plan 008 calibration patch on IMDb KB enrichment helper. Adds Unit-3-layer override that demotes multi_tie -> resolved when top match has overwhelming numVotes dominance over runner-up at the same start_year. Goal: famous-vs-obscure same-title pairs (Roger Rabbit 1988 movie 233k votes vs same-name 1988 video game 210 votes; Mario Galaxy 2026 movie 38k votes vs zero-vote tvEpisodes) resolve to the famous one instead of falling through to the bare-wrapper path.

merged_findings.title: Both start_year=None — same-year guard evaluates None==None as True (override fires)
merged_findings.applied_fix: Added T18 to test_imdb_kb_enrich.sh — locks in current semantics (override fires when both years are None and votes are dominant).

merged_findings.title: not-multi_tie short-circuit branch (top.multi_tie=False) untested in isolation
merged_findings.applied_fix: Added T17 to test_imdb_kb_enrich.sh — asserts helper passes through input unchanged when top.multi_tie=False even with vote-dominant same-year matches.

merged_findings.title: Helper-level idempotency + input non-mutation untested
merged_findings.applied_fix: Added T19 to test_imdb_kb_enrich.sh — verifies input list unmutated after override fires AND second call produces identical outcome.

merged_findings.title: Override has no audit trail — no field on imdb block records 'tie-break applied'
merged_findings.note: Plan 008 explicitly deferred this audit field. Documented in plan's Open Questions section. Re-evaluate if calibration becomes contentious.

merged_findings.title: Override is title-type agnostic — could auto-resolve genuine same-year movie-vs-movie pairs at 10x+ vote spread
merged_findings.note: Plan 008 explicitly skipped a kind/titleType filter (M2 from Unit A discussion). Documented as Phase-2 follow-up if a real example surfaces.

merged_findings.title: T4 Dune integration scenario depends on IMDb DB topology (Lynch 1984 + Villeneuve 2021 staying tier-1 ties)
merged_findings.note: T14 carries the load-bearing year-guard assertion in isolation (synthetic). T4 is integration-layer redundant safety net. Acceptable risk.

merged_findings.title: mk(...) factory copy-pasted across T13/T14/T15/T17/T18/T19 — could DRY
merged_findings.note: Acceptable for v1; on next test-suite refactor extract scripts/tests/fixtures/imdb_kb_enrich_mk.py or collapse into single heredoc with shared factory. Not blocking.

merged_findings.title: Plan 008 System-Wide Impact paragraph claims v1 trusts dataclass shape but code uses defensive `or 0` guards on num_votes — minor plan/code drift
merged_findings.note: Defense-in-depth; lower-risk than removing the guards. Plan 008 could be annotated to acknowledge the guards as belt-and-suspenders. Not blocking.

applied_fixes.summary: Added T17 (not-multi_tie short-circuit), T18 (both years None edge), T19 (idempotency + non-mutation). PASS count grew from 48 to 54.

advisory_findings: merged-001 (None==None year-guard semantics — locked in via T18)
merged-006 (Dune integration test fragility; T14 is durable backup)
residual_risks: TIE_BREAK_VOTE_RATIO=10 boundary untested at exact threshold (9x, 10x, 11x). T13 pins 5x->preserve and T15/T19 pin 200x/1000x->fire; mid-range calibration drift would not be caught.
Real-DB T5/T11 couple suite to current IMDb dataset. Synthetic T13-T19 are the durable safety net.
Override silently swaps tconst without an audit field on the imdb block (deferred per plan 008); post-hoc debugging requires re-running resolve().
testing_gaps_remaining: Threshold boundary (~10x exact) values untested; only sub-threshold (5x) and dominant (200x+) anchored.
No end-to-end test asserts runner.multi_tie remains True after a full resolve() (T15 covers it only at helper level).
verdict_reasoning: Zero P0/P1/P2 findings (one P2 advisory on test factory DRY is acceptable for v1). All P3 findings are advisories (deferred design decisions documented in plan 008) or were resolved by safe_auto test additions. Plan 008 requirements R1-R11 all satisfied; plan-source explicit. Cross-reviewer agreement on the two safe_auto findings (testing + maintainability convergence on missing branch coverage; correctness + testing convergence on None-year semantics) increased confidence in the diagnoses, both of which were applied as test additions.
